title: HA tag enables highly efficient detection of heterologous proteins in Phaeodactylum tricornutum (Pt) exconjugants
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4hta3","text":"Invitrogen's HA tag monoclonal antibody in combination with Western Breeze Anti-Mouse Chemiluminescent Western Blot Immunodetection Kit leads to no background Western blots with Pt lysates. HA tag thus represents a tag of choice for (1) efficient and unambioguous protein expression analysis, (2) immunofluorescence experiments and (3) various IP and pull-down experiments in Pt and possibly other diatoms as well as other marine microeukaryotes.      ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":13,"length":26},{"key":1,"offset":60,"length":75}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"E1B9DD6592014071888F6CD7C3E0B0E9","url":"https://www.thermofisher.com/antibody/product/HA-Tag-Antibody-clone-2-2-2-14-Monoclonal/26183"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"BEC7FE0E3B9F40F88D0EE2F498CD9255","url":"https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/WB7104"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"5uj63","text":"Recommended HA tag nucleotide sequence is as follows: 5'-TATCCGTATGATGTCCCCGATTACGCG-3'.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"b100v","text":"The starting point of this protocol is a genotype+ genetically engineered Pt strain. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":74,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Spin down ~10 7 -10 8 Pt cells for 10 min at 4000 rpm & 10 °C. Keep tubes on ice throughout the protocol.
Resuspend pellets in 50-200 μL cell lysis buffer: 50 mM Tris-HCl, 200 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT, 1 mM PMSF.
Sonicate as follows: 30 sec on / 1 min off for 15 min. We use Bioruptor UCD-200TM.
Use your favorite protein quantification assay. We use ThermoFischer's Bradford assay .
Separate supernatants and pellets/insoluble fractions by centrifuging lysates 45 min at 13500 rpm & 10 °C. Resuspend pellets/insoluble fractions in 100 μL lysis buffer.

Load 10 μL supernatant sample (10 μg protein) and 10 μL pellet/insoluble fraction sample (1 μL "stock"). Load also 6 μL MagicMark protein standard. 1. Prepare MOPS running buffer as follows: 665 mL MQ + 35 mL 20x MOPS buffer. 2. We have had good experience with NuPage 4-12% Bis-Tris 1.5 mm gels with 10 wells . 3. Add 500 μL NuPAGE Antioxidant to the inner reservoir.
Separate proteins for 50 min at 200 V.
Prepare transfer buffer: 40 mL NuPage Transfer Buffer (20x) , 160 mL methanol, 600 mL MQ, 800 μL NuPAGE Antioxidant . We do a wet transfer using XCell II TM Blot Module and PVDF membranes.

Prepare supernatants as follows (40 μL total): 10 μL NuPage LDS Sample Buffer (4x) , 4 μL NuPAGE Sample Reducing Agent (10x) , 40 μg protein, MQ. Prepare pellets/insoluble fractions as follows (40 uL total): 10 μL NuPage LDS Sample Buffer (4x) , 4 μL NuPAGE Sample Reducing Agent (10x) , 4 μL pellet/insoluble fraction suspension, 22 μL MQ. Incubate at 70 °C for 15 min.
Soak ~7 pads in transfer buffer.

Activate PVDF membranes in methanol (~1 min is good). Wash in MQ, leave in transfer buffer. Leave filter papers that come with each membrane soaking in transfer buffer while you're activating membranes.
Transfer proteins for 1 h at 30 V.
Dissassemble your gels and stack a transfer sandwich. If transferring from 2 gels simultaneously, we recommend assembling your sandwich as follows: a
Cut top right corner of each membrane to track membrane faces containing proteins.
Wash each membrane twice with 20 mL MQ for 5 min. All washing and incubation steps at RT were performed on a rocking platform set to 1 (very gentle rocking).
Prapare blocking buffer. For 2 membranes: 10 mL MQ, 4 mL "Part A", 6 mL "Part B".
Block at least 1 h at room temperature. Use 10 mL/membrane. The result in this protocol was obtained after a 2 day blocking step at 4 °C followed by another ~1 h 15 min RT incubation period.
Wash each membrane twice with 20 mL MQ for 5 min.
Prepare 1:10000 dilution of HA tag monoclonal antibody : 14 mL MQ, 4 mL "Part A", 2 mL "Part B", 2 µL antibody stock.
Use 10 mL antibody solution per membrane and incubate ~3 hr.
Prepare 160 mL wash solution: 150 mL MQ + 10 mL Wash Solution (16x). Wash each membrane 4 times in 20 mL for 5 min.
Incubate each membrane in 10 mL anti-mouse secondary antibody solution for 1 hr.
Prepare 160 mL wash solution: 150 mL MQ + 10 mL Wash Solution (16x). Wash each membrane 4 times in 20 mL for 5 min.
Wash each membrane 3 times in 20 mL MQ for 2 min.
Remove excess liquid, place a membrane on transparent plastic foil and cover it evenly with 2 mL Novex CDP-Star alkaline phosphatase substrate.
Cover with alufoil and incubate ~5 min.
Remove excess liquid and cover with another transparent plastic sheet.
We use LI-COR's C-DiGit Blot Scanner .
5 genotype+ Pt exconjugants were screened for protein-HA expression. The left and right image below were obtained with superntants and insoluble fractions, respectively. a
creator: Jernej Turnšek
